subject: Computer Networking
title: IP: The Internet's Addressing System
description: A 60-second lesson on IP: The Internet's Addressing System.

hook.text: Every packet on the internet carries two addresses: where it's from and where it's going. That's IP.

buildup.text: IP stands for Internet Protocol. It handles addressing and routing — nothing more.

discovery.text: IP doesn't guarantee delivery or order. It just labels each packet and sends it toward the destination.

twist.text: Higher protocols like TCP add reliability on top. IP stays simple on purpose — complexity belongs elsewhere.

climax.text: Every device, phone, server, and smart fridge needs an IP address to participate on the internet.

punchline.text: IP puts an address on every packet. That's its only job.
